title: The Anxiety-Tinnitus Feedback Loop and How to Break It
phrases: "We are not our thoughts, but we are responsible for our relationship to them."
"You cannot think your way into a felt sense of safety. The body has its own logic."
"There's a difference between being alone and being with yourself. One is circumstance. The other is practice."
researchers: Josef Rauschecker (Georgetown neuroscientist, tinnitus brain mapping)

title: CBT for Tinnitus What the Evidence Actually Shows
phrases: "The wellness industry sells solutions to problems it helps you believe you have."
"You don't arrive at peace. You stop walking away from it."
"What we call 'the present moment' is not a place you go. It's the only place you've ever been."
"Sit with it long enough and even the worst feeling reveals its edges."
interjections: Bear with me on this one.

title: Acceptance and Commitment Therapy for Tinnitus Distress
phrases: "The question is never whether the pain will come. The question is whether you'll meet it with presence or with narrative."
"The self you're trying to improve is the same self doing the improving. Notice the circularity."
"The most sophisticated defense mechanism is the one that looks like wisdom."
"You cannot think your way into a felt sense of safety. The body has its own logic."

title: How Catastrophic Thinking Amplifies Tinnitus Suffering
phrases: "There is no version of growth that doesn't involve the dissolution of something you thought was permanent."
"Complexity is the ego's favorite hiding place."
"The body remembers what the mind would prefer to file away."
"The paradox of acceptance is that nothing changes until you stop demanding that it does."
interjections: Think about that for a second.

title: The Role of Attention in Tinnitus Perception
